title: Living in Clayton, MO — Complete Guide
content:
Clayton is an upscale urban suburb serving as St. Louis County's business and financial center. Blends high-rise buildings with diverse architecture from historic brick to modern farmhouse.

Housing: K-M for homes. Townhome-style detached homes common. Condos K-M. Premium buildings like The Plaza offer doorman service, pools, fitness. HOA fees -,000+/month.

Key Neighborhoods: The Moorlands (condos, lofts, skyline views), Hi-Pointe-DeMun Historic District (1920s architecture, National Register), Claverach Park (1922, Tudor/Dutch Colonial/Georgian), Downtown Clayton (county government seat, restaurants, retail), Davis Place (K-M, 1930s homes). Others: Brentmoor, Carrswold, Ellenwood, Forest Ridge, Hillcrest, Parkside, Skinker Heights, Southmoor, Wydown Forest, Wydown Terrace.

History: Emerged when STL City separated from County in 1877. Named after Ralph Clayton who donated land for courthouse. Incorporated February 1913. 80% residential despite bustling business district.

Parks: Shaw Park (aquatic center, tennis, pickleball, baseball, sensory garden), Oak Knoll Park (150-year-old Post Oaks, pond, summer concerts). Direct access to Forest Park's 1,300 acres.

Dining: Shasha's Wine Bar, Kaldi's Coffee, 801 Chophouse, City Coffee Creperie, Casa Don Alfonso, Bar Moro, Kingside Diner. Shopping: Ladue Crossing, Saint Louis Galleria nearby.

Schools: Clayton High School ranks 4th in Missouri (U.S. News). Median household income: ,158. Annual Saint Louis Art Fair in September downtown.

Development: Fontbonne University (16 acres) closes 2025. Forsythia on the Park (38 luxury condos near Shaw Park). Douglas Properties luxury condos north of downtown.
